Flags:
- ``fuzzy``: translations suggested by the babel library;
- ``ai_translation``: translations generated by an IA.

## Files

| File                                                                      | Format           | Usage                                                                   |
|---------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| [`/scripts/i18n/i18n_update.py`](../../scripts/i18n/i18n_update.py)       | text / Python    | The script to update translations.                                      |
| [`/scripts/i18n/babel.cfg`](../../src/common/i18n/babel.cfg)              | text / INI       | The configuration used by Babel to extraction the strings to translate. |
| `/locale/messages.pot`                                                    | text / gettext   | The Portable Object Template, common to all the translations.           |
| `/locale/<lang>/LC_MESSAGES/messages.po`                                  | text / gettext   | The Portable Object of translation `<lang>`.                            |
| `/locale/<lang>/LC_MESSAGES/messages.mo`                                  | binary / gettext | The Machine Object of translation `<lang>`.                             |
| [`/scripts/i18n/i18n_translate.py`](../../scripts/i18n/i18n_translate.py) | text / Python    | The script to add new translations.                                     |

## Translations (notes for the translators)

Adding a new translation is as simple as translating a file that contains all the strings of the application.

### String tokens

Some of the strings (most of them) contain tokens ; the tokens contextually replaced by the application at run-time and must be kept as-is in the translated strings.

Tokens can be:

- `{token_name}` in Python files:<br/>``{app_name} is so cool!`` > ``{app_name} est trop cool !``<br/>``Note: {note}/20`` > ``Note : {note}/20``
- `%(token_name)s` in template files:<br/>``%(app_name)s is so cool!`` > ``%(app_name)s est trop cool !``<br/>``Note: %(note)d/20`` > ``Note : %(note)d/20``

### Singular/plural

When two strings are needed for singular and plural, the strings to translate look like:

```
msgid "{locales_number} locale is currently available."
msgid_plural "{locales_number} locales are currently available."
msgstr[0] "{locales_number} langue est actuellement disponible."
msgstr[1] "{locales_number} langues sont actuellement disponibles."
```

### Mandatory translations

Some strings require special attention : they are mandatory. They contain the sub-string ``***`` and all the text from the three asterisks must be deleted from the translations:

```
msgid "Unpaired *** FEMALE"
msgstr "Non appariée"
```

### Flags

Some messages in the translation files are flagged:

- ``fuzzy``: the translations are suggested by the babel library, from the other strings found in the file;
- ``ai_translation``: translations generated by an IA.

Example:

```
#, ai_translation, fuzzy
msgid "Tournament [{tournament_uniq_id}] has started."
msgstr "Toernooi [{tournament_uniq_id}] wordt gestart."
```
These messages require a validation from the translators.

In both cases, the flags must be deleted when the proposed translations are validated or corrected.

## Management (notes for the developers)

The script [`scripts/i18n/i18n_check.py`](../../scripts/i18n/i18n_check.py) does all the tasks needed to manage the translations.

### Extracting strings from the sources

Thanks to the configuration defined in [`scripts/babel.cfg`](../../src/common/i18n/babel.cfg), strings to translate are extracted from the sources to the PO template:

- `src/**.py`, `src/web/templates/*.html` > `locale/messages.pot`

### Creating PO files for new languages

Needed folders and files are created for new languages:

- `scripts/i18n/i18n_check.py` > `locale/<locale>/LC_MESSAGES/messages.po`

To add a new language, run `scripts/i18n/i18n_translate.py`.

### Updating PO files

Source modifications are reported to the PO files:

- `locale/messages.pot` > `locale/<locale>/LC_MESSAGES/messages.po`

### Compiling PO files to MO files

The MO binary files (used at run-time) are compiled from the PO text files:

- `locale/<locale>/LC_MESSAGES/messages.po` > `locale/<locale>/LC_MESSAGES/messages.mo`

### Filling missing translations

Initial translations can be provided by models, this can be a good help for translators (this can be done with `i18n_translate.py`).

- [Using models to pre-build new translations](i18n-models.md)
